name: Cosmic XR - Space In Your Room

reviews.review_description:
Update:
The developers have made an effort to correct many of the things that I found fault with in my earlier review. The graphics are improved and many inaccuracies corrected. Although I still can't recommend this for someone interested in a deeper understanding and visual experience of the universe, I find it to be appealing as a learning tool for young people getting their first introduction to astronomy~~~ therefore four stars.

******
This is a cute app that kids might enjoy, but anyone else with an interest in cosmology will be disappointed. The graphics quality is  mediocre. The bands of Jupiter are inaccurate random colored rings. The rings and features of Saturn are completely inaccurate. Earth looks like a cheap globe made in the 50's. Venus is a false color radar map (Venus actually looks nothing like this), but this is not mentioned anywhere. All of the galaxies in the "galaxy view" are just two dimensional little pictures floating around. The models of the Andromeda galaxy and the Milky Way are completely inaccurate, and seem to have been made up without referring to a single reference work.  In every view, you are surrounded by random stars (even in solar system, galaxy and cosmic web view!), and the stars are even visible right through the middle of the black hole! All in all, I think that the colorful but low-resolution and completely inaccurate representations here are only appropriate for kids, who might enjoy this (therefore two stars).

i was blown away in mesmerized about this app however there was some slight bug issues but i'm sure more software update improvements is along the way. i would definitely recommend anybody who's new to the vr world

As others have noted, this isn’t a vast space exploration program. It focuses mostly on our galaxy and the educational material will run out soon. But I really love watching the rotation of our solar system as well as the movement of the galaxies it does show. And the picking up planets was a nice touch (pun intended). It is definitely worth the small price I paid.

I love going to the planetarium and watching the space shows, my favorite part is when they zoom out as far as possible and it really makes you feel very small. I wish they had that feature here. I wish the cosmic Webb was like actually moving and didn’t feel so stationary too. I also wish that the planets came with information on the moons Would be awesome to see more of that added
Probably my favorite app I’ve ever downloaded so far… it is absolutely gorgeous and so immersive￼

it really brings the universe into your space. 


















i enjoyed looking at everything and it's amazing .



















i think the developers did a really great job excellent for stargazers or even kids who are learning about solar system.






















 the colors are brilliant .













i also like the way you can look at the solar system and a certain time in the past or future 





each part is in great detail and very informative i also found it very relaxing
Accurate, factual and facinating.
At first glance, it is beautiful.  Beyond 10 minutes, there wasn't much more to do.
Absolutly fantastic and can only get bigger and better!!!

Gotta disagree with anyone who doesn't like this.  Beautiful renditions.  Especially enjoyed the solar bodies in relative size, and space vehicles.

Sure, certain things could be this or that, but what this app already is, is definitely worth the purchase. I've seen a version of this concept with the Apple VR and this one shuts that one down.

Yu can spend hours exploring our incredible universe, planets and the black hole is absolutely  amazing! This is one of the best apps I've purchased for VR ... All the space and sci fi lovers out there, You have got to try this!

reviews.review_title: Cosmic XR: Learning in Mixed Reality
reviews.review_description:
As an innovative and XR passionate educator, I'm always looking for apps that actually work in a busy classroom. I was instantly sold on Cosmic XR. It's clearly built by people who understand the reality of teaching.

First off, the setup is seamless, truly “plug-and-play”. It’s a huge relief. For example, I could safely manage five students all in close proximity with no fuss, getting clear, real-time oversight of the whole session. The fact that it's in mixed reality (MR) helps so much. Kids stay aware of their real-world surroundings, eliminating that usual VR disorientation and keeping everyone grounded. MR means the digital lesson blends right into the classroom, transforming solo work into genuinely shared, synchronous collaboration, which is exactly how kids learn best, by talking and teaching each other.

Honestly, Cosmic XR is definitely a blueprint for other developers to follow in the education sector. It perfectly balances intuitive control and student safety with powerful, collaborative MR learning…

reviews.review_description: One word for this game PERFECT this is the best game ever just an few thing's Neptune and Jupiter have ring's yes this is true in real life
Love it

I always dream with see planets and galaxies this is amazing well made 5 stars Excellent graphics and great visuals

Beautiful idea. Really well implemented but there's only about ten minutes of content.

Kids might enjoy it.

It’s one of the most beautiful mixed reality experiences I’ve ever had on Quest. I’m not sure why it moved me so deeply, made me emotional perhaps it’s the sheer scale of the universe and the realization of how tiny we are, just like particles drifting through billions of years of cosmic time. It felt almost meditative. I highly recommend it to people of all ages, especially to inspire kids to explore space and science. This is the kind of experience every child should have not mind-numbing endless short videos.
Good experience. But limited. But worth the money.

reviews.review_title: Great but add two things PLEASE.
reviews.review_description: Amazing, but i would LOVE the game so much if they added exploration, even in the cosmic web (as much as possible). But also being able to go onto planets’ surfaces would be so cool. Please look into this review and add things from it if you still update the game.
Goooooooood
Muy chulol

Got this for my space loving niece and it has become her favorite game to play in VR.  I wish we could do some sort of multiplayer experience but perhaps in a future update.  For now I follow along with her casting to my phone and we still have a great time exploring the planets and stars.  Outstanding presentation in MR and it helps her mom when she tries it as most VR only experiences are uncomfortable for her.  I look forward to future updates and content.
This game has so mAnyy cool views

reviews.review_title: Gives perspectife of how small we are
reviews.review_description: Great to learn, hopefully more content coming soon
Great immersive experience for kids

Cosmic XR is a hidden gem in the VR/MR world. I am surprised it is not on top of the charts. It’s like having a magical, interactive planetarium in your home. The mixed reality integration is impressive, letting celestial bodies float around your real space in a way that feels both educational and awe-inspiring. For the price, it’s an incredible value. At the very least, try the trial.

Just dwnloaded and WOW !
Amazing experience. A fantastic experience and a beautiful view of space. Well worth buying and being absorbed in these amazing graphics. Really detailed models of the space station and Mars rover. I am just so excited to have this on my quest.
very well done, serious simulation, and I just dont know how thjs could be bettered?
Super
Good
Basically space engine
realy enjoy this app

Interactive and mesmerizingly fun! This app is on the right track. It does need more content add-ons in the future. 4 stars, the solar system section was not being displayed properly on my Oculus 3. Hand gestures are sensitive and still a bit finicky.

Developer team fixed solar system rendering. Updated to 5 stars.
Not much to do but fun
Space unit

reviews.review_title: I dont know what to say
reviews.review_description: Very fun

I’m in Awe, this looks absolutely amazing in mixed reality, it brings back happy memories of going on a field trip to the planetarium 30+ yrs ago but with this it’s literally at your fingertips 🤯 there are other apps on here that I have tried and they don’t even come close to this, imo anyways but I would love to see higher resolutions on the planets, when you expand them all the way they get super blurry but don’t get me wrong it still looks good, just not as crisp as default size
There is not much you can do, you can see everything in about 15 minutes
This is a wonderful app to experience the world of space interactively. I hope more content will be added in time.

This app is incredible, truly. If you have any passing interest in space, astronomy, science, meditation, or just realizing your tiny place in the vastness of the universe, you need to experience this app. It makes terrific use of hand tracking and mix reality as well. Bravo to the dev!!! 👏👏👏

reviews.review_title: Great Job. Looking forward to more.
reviews.review_description: For the first version, this is amazing. 4 stars for that. I love the cosmic scale. I always recommend that any developers look to the Cosmic Watch app as the gold standard for understanding the celestial mechanics of our own solar system. This app has great potential for that. I would love to see more features within our solar system. For example choosing a heliocentric or a geocentric view of planetary movement where the geocentric view can be from the ecliptic or topocentric. The latter brilliantly shows how the Earth's tilt and axial wobble impact our earthly experience of the planets and stars.  I also think the ability to speed and slow time while tracing orbital paths is essential so you can observe the incredible geometry that emerges.
Fake reviews dont buy, total rip off

Wow..honestly just wow! So beautifully created, factually very informative and the visuals are mind blowing. I could sit for hours just enjoying being amongst the stars, planets and other galaxies. 
Can’t wait for more updates and new items.
The most amazing experience, blew my mind. Educational and fun, great value, a must have.
Noice
space... YES

This app is a must for anyone who loves the cosmos — especially if you want to visualize our nearby space environments right in your own room. The Galaxies mode is also one of the most beautiful augmented reality experiences I’ve ever seen.

And here comes the but, and the reason I didn’t give it five stars: there’s a bug in the Planetarium. When you select a planet that has moons or orbiting objects — like Jupiter or Mars — these often disappear, leaving only the names of the moons/asteroids visible.

Apart from this issue, the app is excellent. If this gets fixed, I’d happily update my review to five stars. Right now, it’s a solid 4.5 for me.

An app that is wonderfully thought out, and has the potential to expand the mind. Just to have the scale of the universe is enough to make you think long and hard. I would love some narration and a few interesting facts in the future. 6 Stars.

Putting the universe at your fingertips is very humbling. Perspective is everything and this app gets it right. My only beef is using the grab controlls can be weird sometimes. A must have in your vr arsenal.
I like the game
